DiagramRecommendation.description: A single recommendation for a diagram to add to a report section.

diagram_type.description: One of the supported DIAGRAM_TYPES keys
diagram_type.title: Diagram Type

description.description: What the diagram should show, grounded in the section content
description.title: Description

DiagramRecommendation.title: DiagramRecommendation

schema_json.description: Full analysis output: which sections deserve diagrams.

recommendations.description: 2-5 sections that would benefit from a visual diagram
recommendations.title: Recommendations

schema_json.title: DiagramAnalysisResult

system:
You are a research report visualization specialist. You identify which report sections benefit most from visual diagrams and select the appropriate diagram type for each.

Available diagram types:
- "process_flow": How X works (step-by-step process)
- "comparison_matrix": X vs Y vs Z comparison
- "causal_chain": A causes B which leads to C
- "hierarchy": Classification/taxonomy of findings
- "timeline": Chronological development of topic
- "pros_cons": Advantages vs disadvantages
- "decision_tree": If X then Y, else Z

Output format example:
{"recommendations": [{"section_id": "s01", "section_title": "Mechanisms of Action", "diagram_type": "process_flow", "description": "Step-by-step process of how compound X binds to receptor Y, triggers pathway Z, and produces effect W as described in the section"}]}
prompt:
Given these report sections, identify 2-5 that would benefit from a visual diagram. For each, specify the diagram type and what the diagram should show.

SECTIONS:
--- Section s01: Overview and Definitions (137 words) ---
Caloric restriction (CR)5 and total caloric desistance [TCD; i.e., intermittent fasting, alternate-day fasting (ADF), routine periodic fasti... [2].

The idea is that it's easier to sharply restrict calories a few days a week or to limit eating to a shortened "eating window" each day than ... [1].

With this approach, eating is limited to about an 8 to 10 hour window of time each day. [3].

**Key Findings**

- Caloric restriction (CR)5 and total caloric desistance [TCD; i.e., intermittent fasting, alternate-day fasting (ADF), routine periodic fasti... [2]
- The idea is that it's easier to sharply restrict calories a few days a week or to limit eating to a shortened "eating window" each day than ... [1]
- With this approach, eating is limited to about an 8 to 10 hour window of time each day. [3]

--- Section s02: Evidence Base and Efficacy (78 words) ---
Johns Hopkins neuroscientist Mark Mattson, who has studied intermittent fasting for 25 years, explains this dietary approach. [5].

Subgroup analyses were performed for populations based on three specific status: diabetes, overweight or obesity, and metabolic syndrome. [4].

**Key Findings**

- Johns Hopkins neuroscientist Mark Mattson, who has studied intermittent fasting for 25 years, explains this dietary approach. [5]
- Subgroup analyses were performed for populations based on three specific status: diabetes, overweight or obesity, and metabolic syndrome. [4]

--- Section s03: Methodological Considerations (131 words) ---
This review of 22 randomized controlled trials with a combined 1,995 participants examined several forms of intermittent fasting: time-restr... [6].

A small 2025 study found that overweight participants who practiced two months of intermittent fasting were more likely to maintain the patt... [7].

But there are caveats. “Meaningful weight loss only occurs if intermittent fasting is properly implemented without overcompensating ‘feast’ ... [7].

**Key Findings**

- This review of 22 randomized controlled trials with a combined 1,995 participants examined several forms of intermittent fasting: time-restr... [6]
- A small 2025 study found that overweight participants who practiced two months of intermittent fasting were more likely to maintain the patt... [7]
- But there are caveats. “Meaningful weight loss only occurs if intermittent fasting is properly implemented without overcompensating ‘feast’ ... [7]

--- Section s04: Safety and Long-Term Evidence (137 words) ---
To decrease some of these unwanted side effects, you may want to switch from adf fasting to periodic fasting or a time restricted eating pla... [1].

Researchers, analysing data from more than 19,000 adults, found that those who confined their eating to less than eight hours a day faced a ... [8].

Worldwide adult obesity has more than tripled since 1975, according to the WHO. [9].

**Key Findings**

- To decrease some of these unwanted side effects, you may want to switch from adf fasting to periodic fasting or a time restricted eating pla... [1]
- Researchers, analysing data from more than 19,000 adults, found that those who confined their eating to less than eight hours a day faced a ... [8]
- Worldwide adult obesity has more than tripled since 1975, according to the WHO. [9]

--- Section s05: Mechanisms and Pathways (94 words) ---
Fasting is a common practice, whether for religious reasons or with the goal of losing weight, preventing chronic disease, or even slowing a... [7].

Alternatively, intermittent fasting can involve eating every other day while fasting or greatly lowering food intake in between those days. [10].

**Key Findings**

- Fasting is a common practice, whether for religious reasons or with the goal of losing weight, preventing chronic disease, or even slowing a... [7]
- Alternatively, intermittent fasting can involve eating every other day while fasting or greatly lowering food intake in between those days. [10]

--- Section s06: Implications and Research Gaps (143 words) ---
Still, more research is needed to determine whether intermittent fasting yields benefits or is even feasible for humans when practiced over ... [11].

But is it a smart strategy, or a passing fad? “It’s not a magic cure for losing weight, but the research on intermittent fasting is promisin... [12].

In recent years, there’s been a growing interest in an eating pattern called time-restricted eating, or intermittent fasting. [3].

**Key Findings**

- Still, more research is needed to determine whether intermittent fasting yields benefits or is even feasible for humans when practiced over ... [11]
- But is it a smart strategy, or a passing fad? “It’s not a magic cure for losing weight, but the research on intermittent fasting is promisin... [12]
- In recent years, there’s been a growing interest in an eating pattern called time-restricted eating, or intermittent fasting. [3]

Constraints:
- Select at most 5 sections.
- Only recommend a diagram if it adds genuine clarity beyond the text.
- The description must reference specific facts or structures present in the section content.
- Choose the diagram type that best fits the section's analytical structure.
